input.content:
No changes produced.

This bead (ddx-64b9a198) is an epic whose sole acceptance criterion is "All child beads closed. Locked decisions from the planning round honored." It carries no testable work of its own — the testable ACs live in its children.

Children (label story:11), all currently open:
- ddx-25069ce4 P3 agentmetrics: rollup-engine TD (filed only)
- ddx-2bb01b91 P2 agentmetrics: GraphQL agentMetrics(window, groupBy) resolver + revision-keyed cache
- ddx-4deb0a8e P2 availability: Availability/Performance section toggle on /providers
- ddx-9fea5624 P2 agentmetrics: internal/agentmetrics package over storage-abstracted loader
- ddx-c62a8223 P3 availability: sparkline column + FEAT-021 dashboard tile

What is done: nothing — epic-level rollup only.
What is blocking: closure depends on the five open children above; epic cannot close while any child is open.
What a follow-up attempt would need: drain the children first (queue them through execute-loop). Once all five are closed and the locked planning decisions are reflected, this epic auto-satisfies and can be closed without code changes.

Recommendation to the orchestrator: do not re-queue this epic for execution. Close it administratively after the last child closes, or leave it for a final rollup pass. Escalating to a stronger model will not change the outcome — there is no implementation surface here.

data.text: No changes. This bead is a roll-up epic with five open children carrying the actual ACs (ddx-25069ce4, ddx-2bb01b91, ddx-4deb0a8e, ddx-9fea5624, ddx-c62a8223). The epic auto-satisfies once those close; no implementation surface exists here. Rationale written to `.ddx/executions/20260502T115426-4c65b442/no_changes_rationale.txt`.
